active_answer: if you lose control or drop your partner since their head is likely to be the first thing to hit the ground
active_question: Why does Esinem describe inversions as carrying a high risk of serious injury?
edited_answer: Losing control or dropping an inverted partner may send their head into the ground first, creating a high risk of serious injury.
edited_question: Why does Esinem describe inversions as carrying a high risk of serious injury?
paraphrase_rationale: The original answer was a dependent if-clause; the revision preserves the same source-supported injury mechanism as a grammatical, self-contained warning.
reason: The original answer was a dependent if-clause; the revision preserves the same source-supported injury mechanism as a grammatical, self-contained warning.
support_evidence: Inversions carry a high risk of serious injury if you lose control or drop your partner since their head is likely to be the first thing to hit the ground. With this in mind, you will learn how to split the load with the gote for greater safety, potential comfort and, most importantly, to give you the confidence that your partner won’t meet the floor head first.
